betterquesting:10.desc:8:
There is so much in the universe that is just great. So much that brings pleasure to the senses.
And yet, if this pleasure becomes a goal, a pursuit, the objective of life, loses its pleasure.
The feeling of a cool, refreshing ice cream on a hot day. An unbeatable moment.
If that moment is relived, over and over, though, it loses its appeal, it becomes duller and duller every time it is experienced.
And soon enough, a life that was full of pleasure turns into a gray and meaningless one.
The attraction of food, of drink. That is a simple one.
The one that is more subtle is the one for beauty, art, nature. That too, it can fade away if overdulged. 
Adiction to beauty, it too can take the pleasure of beauty away. It can strip it of its meaning. And just like that, by seeking beauty, beauty is taken away.
A world that confuses pleasure for meaning. That is the fight the Lord of Gluttony must fight.
My mission, ensuring that moderation lives in the hearts of all.
Because it is this moderation that sets us free from the chains of waning pleasure, of addiction, of abuse.
My mission, fighting excess.
One, I think, I am ready to start pursuing. Now.
